findings.message: Agent has code templates and workflow guides but no dedicated ## 示例交互 section showing user/agent interaction pairs; -5 for one-or-fewer examples
Agent has zero interaction examples; no section showing user/agent request-response flow for deployment, CI/CD, or infrastructure tasks
Agent lacks a dedicated output-format section; no specification of expected response structure for deployment plans, pipeline configs, or incident reports
Vague quantifiers (-18): 适当 x3, 清晰 x2, 合理 x2, 良好 x1 — implementation guidelines lack measurable thresholds
Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 清晰 x3, 全面 x2, 具体 x2, 直观 x1, 现实的 x1 — requirement descriptions lack measurable acceptance criteria
Vague quantifiers (-18): 全面 x4, 清晰 x2, 彻底 x1, 有效 x1 — test quality standards use unmeasurable adjectives
Agent has zero interaction examples; no section showing user/agent request-response flow for architecture decisions or code reviews
Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 清晰 x4, 合理 x3, 详细 x3, 完整 x2, 及时 x1 — instructions lack measurable criteria
README.md links to '../../docs/THREAD_AGENT_INTEGRATION.md' and '../../docs/THREAD_CONTEXT_ISOLATION_DESIGN.md' but docs/ is absent from package.json 'files' field; links broken in installed packages
Command invokes mcp__thread-manager__create_thread but declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
Role-mapping table references '.claude/agents/tech_leader.md' (underscore) but actual file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); /start-task tl silently loads wrong agent
start-task.md and tl-start.md both reference 'tech_leader.md' (underscore) while agent file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); tech-lead role silently broken across two commands
Command invokes multiple mcp__thread-manager__* tools but declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
Command invokes mcp__thread-manager__list_threads but declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
Step 3 instructs reading '.claude/agents/tech_leader.md' (underscore) but actual file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); /tl-start silently fails to load tech-leader persona

UserPromptSubmit hook captures every user prompt and writes it to local SQLite DB via record-message.js; expected thread-manager feature but the data-capture behavior is not disclosed prominently during installation
Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 完整 x3, 清晰 x3, 合理 x2, 适当 x2, 专业 x2 — design quality criteria are subjective and not actionable
Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 清晰 x3, 专业 x2, 完整 x2, 准确 x2 — generated document quality criteria are unverifiable
SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; missing required 'name' and 'description' fields causing the skill not to register; all thread-based commands depend on it
thread-manager SKILL.md missing frontmatter so skill is unregistered; all thread-based commands and agents depend on its MCP tools but the dependency is invisible to the registry

spawnSync called with shell:true at lines 168, 176, and 188; args are currently hardcoded so injection is not immediately exploitable, but shell:true on an array invocation is unnecessary and removes a safety layer

Comment on line 4 documents curl|bash installation pattern; script is explicitly designed to be fetched from a remote URL and piped into bash with no integrity verification, creating a supply-chain attack surface

'postinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/postinstall.js automatically on every npm install; actual content prints a banner (low actual risk) but the unconditional execution hook remains active
'preinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/preinstall.js automatically on every npm install
@xenova/transformers ^2.17.2 and puppeteer ^24.31.0 use caret ranges; automatic minor/patch upgrades can introduce vulnerabilities or breaking changes without explicit opt-in
puppeteer dependency downloads a ~300 MB Chromium browser binary on npm install; users are unlikely to expect this implicit large binary dependency

Shell variable $thread_prefix is directly interpolated into SQLite query string with no sanitization; malicious input such as "' OR '1'='1'; DELETE FROM threads; --" could corrupt the threads database
Appends shell function aliases to ~/.zshrc without per-session confirmation; modifies user shell profile outside the repository
Copies agent/command/CLAUDE.md files to ~/.claude/ and creates backup directories in user's home directory outside the project repo
Role-mapping table references '.claude/agents/tech_leader.md' (underscore) but actual file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); /start-task tl silently loads wrong agent
Step 3 instructs reading '.claude/agents/tech_leader.md' (underscore) but actual file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); /tl-start silently fails to load tech-leader persona
SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; missing required 'name' and 'description' fields causing the skill not to register; all thread-based commands depend on it

Comment on line 4 documents curl|bash installation pattern; script is explicitly designed to be fetched from a remote URL and piped into bash with no integrity verification, creating a supply-chain attack surface

Shell variable $thread_prefix is directly interpolated into SQLite query string with no sanitization; malicious input such as "' OR '1'='1'; DELETE FROM threads; --" could corrupt the threads database

'postinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/postinstall.js automatically on every npm install; actual content prints a banner (low actual risk) but the unconditional execution hook remains active
'preinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/preinstall.js automatically on every npm install

spawnSync called with shell:true at lines 168, 176, and 188; args are currently hardcoded so injection is not immediately exploitable, but shell:true on an array invocation is unnecessary and removes a safety layer
Copies agent/command/CLAUDE.md files to ~/.claude/ and creates backup directories in user's home directory outside the project repo
Appends shell function aliases to ~/.zshrc without per-session confirmation; modifies user shell profile outside the repository

UserPromptSubmit hook captures every user prompt and writes it to local SQLite DB via record-message.js; expected thread-manager feature but the data-capture behavior is not disclosed prominently during installation
@xenova/transformers ^2.17.2 and puppeteer ^24.31.0 use caret ranges; automatic minor/patch upgrades can introduce vulnerabilities or breaking changes without explicit opt-in
puppeteer dependency downloads a ~300 MB Chromium browser binary on npm install; users are unlikely to expect this implicit large binary dependency
